title: Analysts Reset Bloom Energy as Stock Explodes Higher
text: Following Bloom Energy's exceptional Q1 2026 earnings and stock price surge, multiple Wall Street analysts reset their ratings and price targets to reflect changing valuation dynamics. While maintaining predominantly bullish outlooks, analysts acknowledge valuation concerns at current price levels. Consensus rating from 20 analysts includes 20% Strong Buy, 30% Buy, 45% Hold, 5% Sell, and 0% Strong Sell. Price targets range from low $150s to high $300s reflecting divergent views on fair valuation. BTIG and Evercore ISI raised price targets to $295, citing record first-quarter results and strong AI data center demand. RBC Capital maintains $335 price target, representing most bullish outlook. Barclays raised to $254 while maintaining Equal Weight rating, suggesting fair valuation at elevated growth multiples. Average analyst price target around $240-$260 range reflects measured approach to current premium valuations. Analysts widely acknowledge exceptional business execution and growth trajectory while emphasizing execution risks and limited margin of safety at current multiples. The consensus view supports long-term AI infrastructure thesis while cautioning against excessive near-term valuation expansion.
